description: Build a Swift CLI (proposed slug `swift-human-visual-verify-cli`) that takes a release-receipts directory + an artifact path/URL, opens the artifact for the human (open URL in browser via mcp__claude-in-chrome or the local file in the appropriate macOS app), prompts for verdict (matches-expectation | wrong-content | partial-issue | could-not-verify), captures notes (mandatory for wrong-content / partial-issue per the schema invariant), optionally captures a screenshot, then writes a HumanVisualVerification entry into the appropriate ReleaseOperationItem via JSON edit. The CLI is the actual enforcement product behind the schema we just added. Operator directive: 'we need to productize this' after the vapor-wares.com near-miss. Schema reference: HumanVisualVerification + HumanVisualVerificationVerdict + LaunchGate.requiresHumanVisualVerification in release-operations-schemas.
resolutionNote: Reframed by operator 2026-05-14: 'a human can click a button! that's all we need. We need to in our release apps, schedule a notification for a human to open the app and click. and then we need to make the UI for the things that the humans release.' CLI is the wrong shape — humans should never type a verification command. Replaced by three new beads: release-verification-ui-package (button + verdict capture SwiftUI), release-verification-notifications (UNUserNotificationCenter scheduling per release item), release-artifact-viewer-components (per-artifact-type SwiftUI views the human sees before clicking).
title: Productize HumanVisualVerification as a Swift CLI that loops the human through the verdict
